heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Note:** This section consolidates all falsifiability criteria. Duplicate sections have been removed. The α = 0.05 threshold is consistent across all core documents.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (single placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time).
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Note:** The OSF ID placeholder `osf.io/TBD` must be replaced with a reserved ID (e.g., `osf.io/abcde`) prior to enrollment start. The pre-registration date is provisional and will be updated upon OSF reservation.
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** Between-group difference in PAM-13 change score (Δ) from baseline to 6 months
- **Analysis plan:** Independent two-sample t-test (two-sided); ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity
- **Secondary outcomes:** Medication adherence (MMAS-8), quality of life (EQ-5D-5L), healthcare utilisation (number of hospitalisations)
- **Exploratory outcomes:** Patient activation trajectory (mixed-effects model), LLM engagement metrics (session count, message length)

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
- **Formula:** n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** Z_α/2 = 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-sided), Z_β = 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- **Effect size δ:** 5.4 points (PAM-13 MCID)
- **Standard deviation σ:** 10 (assumed — see justification below)
- **Calculation:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2 · 10² / 5.4² = 7.84 · 200 / 29.16 ≈ 53.8 → **n = 55 per group**
- **Total N:** 110 + 20% dropout = **132 participants**
- **Sensitivity analysis (required N per group):**
  - σ = 8: n ≈ 35
  - σ = 10: n ≈ 55 (base case)
  - σ = 12: n ≈ 78
- **Justification for σ = 10:** Based on prior PAM-13 studies (Hibbard et al., 2004, PMID 15333167; Health Serv Res 39(4 Pt 1):1005–26), standard deviation of PAM-13 scores in similar populations ranges from 8 to 12; σ = 10 is the midpoint. This will be updated after pilot data collection.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Low recruitment (N < 132) | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment; extended enrollment window; contingency budget for advertising |
| 2 | High dropout rate (>20%) | Medium | High | Retention protocol: weekly reminders, compensation, flexible scheduling; per-protocol sensitivity analysis |
| 3 | LLM technical failure (downtime >48h) | Low | Medium | Redundant server; fallback to standard care; monitoring dashboard |
| 4 | Adverse events >5% | Low | High | DSMB monthly review; stopping rule; immediate unblinding if SAE |
| 5 | PAM-13 ceiling effect (baseline >80) | Medium | Medium | Stratified enrollment by baseline activation; sensitivity analysis excluding high baseline |
| 6 | Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | Encryption at rest/transit; access logs; IRB-approved data management plan; GDPR compliance |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Single-centre design:** Results may not generalise to other healthcare systems or populations.
- **Short follow-up (6 months):** Long-term sustainability of patient activation improvements is not assessed.
- **Self-reported primary outcome (PAM-13):** Subject to social desirability bias and recall bias.
- **Unblinded intervention:** Participants and care coordinators are aware of group assignment; potential for performance bias.
- **Placebo effect:** No sham LLM intervention; control group receives standard care only.
- **Sample size assumptions:** Standard deviation σ = 10 is based on prior studies; actual variability may differ.
- **LLM version drift:** Model updates during the trial may affect intervention consistency; version freeze planned.
- **Digital literacy bias:** Participants with low digital literacy may be underrepresented; exclusion criteria may limit generalisability.
- **Missing data:** Dropout and incomplete PAM-13 responses will be handled via multiple imputation; sensitivity analyses will assess robustness.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead institution:** [Institution Name TBD] — Principal Investigator, trial coordination, data analysis
- **Clinical site:** [Hospital/Clinic Name TBD] — Patient recruitment, intervention delivery, data collection
- **LLM development partner:** [Company/University Name TBD] — AI model fine-tuning, deployment, monitoring
- **Biostatistics core:** [Department/Unit Name TBD] — Sample size calculation, interim analysis, final analysis
- **Data safety monitoring board (DSMB):** [Members TBD] — Independent safety monitoring, stopping rule evaluation
- **Regulatory/ethics advisor:** [Name TBD] — IRB submission, GDPR compliance, adverse event reporting
- **Patient advisory group:** [Patient representatives TBD] — Study design input, consent form review, dissemination
